journal_entry.reason: The meal cost for factory production staff is manufacturing overhead, which is a product cost capitalized to work-in-progress inventory under IAS 2. The corporate credit card payment creates a liability. Debit inventory (work in progress) to capitalize the manufacturing overhead cost, and credit the credit card payable liability.

output_debit_classifier.reason: This transaction records a manufacturing overhead cost incurred via corporate credit card. The meal provided to factory production staff is explicitly classified as manufacturing overhead, making it a product cost rather than a period expense. Under IAS 2, manufacturing overhead must be capitalized to inventory (work in progress) as part of the cost of production. The use of a corporate credit card creates a liability that will be settled when the credit card bill is paid. The entry debits work-in-progress inventory to capitalize the overhead cost and credits the credit card payable liability.
